017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.observation;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
021
022import java.time.*;
023
024import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.metrics.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.tracing.*;
026
027/**
028 * Factory for custom (non-request) {@link Observation}s, composing a {@link MetricsRecorder} and a
029 * {@link TracerHook}.
030 *
031 * <p>
032 * This is the explicit programmatic observation substrate for {@code juneau-rest-server}: it reuses the
033 * exact same two SPIs the request path uses ({@link MetricsRecorder}, {@link TracerHook}) and the same
034 * {@code metricName} / {@code metricTags} comma-separated tag model, but is decoupled from
035 * {@code RestRequest} so application code can instrument an arbitrary block of work. Juneau deliberately
036 * has no method-interception / AOP substrate, so observation is explicit (a try-with-resources block)
037 * rather than annotation-driven &mdash; consistent with the framework's explicit-over-magic philosophy.
038 *
039 * <h5 class='topic'>No-backend contract</h5>
040 *
041 * <p>
042 * An {@code Observer} built from the no-op SPIs ({@link #NOOP}) never takes a timestamp and hands back
043 * {@link Observation#NOOP}, so an observation in an unconfigured process costs nothing beyond the
044 * try-with-resources scaffolding. {@link #start(String, String)} short-circuits to the no-op handle
045 * whenever <i>both</i> backends are the no-op singletons.
046 *
047 * <h5 class='topic'>Wiring</h5>
048 *
049 * <p>
050 * Typically obtained from a {@code RestContext}'s bean store &mdash; register the same
051 * {@code @Bean MetricsRecorder} / {@code @Bean TracerHook} you already register for request-path
052 * observability, then build an {@code Observer} from them (or install one as the {@link Observations}
053 * default). Construct directly for tests:
054 *
055 * <p class='bjava'>
056 *    Observer <jv>o</jv> = <jk>new</jk> Observer(<jk>new</jk> MicrometerMetricsRecorder(<jv>registry</jv>), <jk>new</jk> OtelTracerHook(<jv>otel</jv>));
057 * </p>
058 *
059 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
060 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Observations}
061 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Observation}
062 *    <li class='jc'>{@link MetricsRecorder}
063 *    <li class='jc'>{@link TracerHook}
064 * </ul>
065 *
066 * @since 10.0.0
067 */
068@SuppressWarnings({
069   "resource" // start(...) returns the Observation for the caller's try-with-resources; ActiveObservation holds the tracer Scope it closes in its own close().
070})
071public final class Observer {
072
073   /**
074    * Observer backed by the no-op metrics recorder and tracer &mdash; produces only
075    * {@link Observation#NOOP}.
076    */
077   public static final Observer NOOP = new Observer(NoOpMetricsRecorder.INSTANCE, NoOpTracerHook.INSTANCE);
078
079   // Constant name intentionally uses camelCase to match observation/metrics naming conventions.
080   @SuppressWarnings("java:S115")
081   private static final String ARG_recorder = "recorder";
082   // Constant name intentionally uses camelCase to match observation/metrics naming conventions.
083   @SuppressWarnings("java:S115")
084   private static final String ARG_tracer = "tracer";
085   // Constant name intentionally uses camelCase to match observation/metrics naming conventions.
086   @SuppressWarnings("java:S115")
087   private static final String ARG_name = "name";
088
089   private final MetricsRecorder recorder;
090   private final TracerHook tracer;
091   private final boolean active;
092
093   /**
094    * Constructor.
095    *
096    * @param recorder The metrics recorder to time observations against. Must not be <jk>null</jk>;
097    *    use {@link NoOpMetricsRecorder#INSTANCE} to disable metrics.
098    * @param tracer The tracer to open spans against. Must not be <jk>null</jk>; use
099    *    {@link NoOpTracerHook#INSTANCE} to disable tracing.
100    */
101   public Observer(MetricsRecorder recorder, TracerHook tracer) {
102      this.recorder = assertArgNotNull(ARG_recorder, recorder);
103      this.tracer = assertArgNotNull(ARG_tracer, tracer);
104      this.active = recorder != NoOpMetricsRecorder.INSTANCE || tracer != NoOpTracerHook.INSTANCE;
105   }
106
107   /**
108    * Starts a new observation.
109    *
110    * <p>
111    * When both backends are the no-op singletons this returns {@link Observation#NOOP} without taking a
112    * timestamp or opening a span. Otherwise it opens a span via {@link TracerHook#startSpan(String)} and
113    * captures a start timestamp; the paired timer sample is recorded on {@link Observation#close()}.
114    *
115    * @param name The observation name &mdash; used as both the span name and the metric timer name.
116    *    Must not be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
117    * @param tags Additional metric tags as comma-separated {@code key=value} pairs (e.g.
118    *    {@code "team=payments,region=us-east"}), or <jk>null</jk> / empty for none.
119    * @return The started {@link Observation}. Never <jk>null</jk>.
120    */
121   public Observation start(String name, String tags) {
122      assertArgNotNullOrBlank(ARG_name, name);
123      if (! active)
124         return Observation.NOOP;
125      var scope = tracer.startSpan(name);
126      return new ActiveObservation(recorder, scope, name, tags == null ? "" : tags);
127   }
128
129   /**
130    * Returns whether this observer has at least one live backend (metrics or tracing).
131    *
132    * @return <jk>true</jk> if either the metrics recorder or the tracer is non-no-op.
133    */
134   public boolean isActive() {
135      return active;
136   }
137
138   private static final class ActiveObservation implements Observation {
139
140      private final MetricsRecorder recorder;
141      private final Scope scope;
142      private final String name;
143      private final String tags;
144      private final long startNanos;
145      private Throwable error;
146
147      ActiveObservation(MetricsRecorder recorder, Scope scope, String name, String tags) {
148         this.recorder = recorder;
149         this.scope = scope;
150         this.name = name;
151         this.tags = tags;
152         this.startNanos = System.nanoTime();
153      }
154
155      @Override /* Observation */
156      public void setError(Throwable error) {
157         this.error = error;
158      }
159
160      @Override /* Observation */
161      public void close() {
162         var elapsed = Duration.ofNanos(System.nanoTime() - startNanos);
163         try {
164            if (nn(error))
165               scope.setError(error);
166         } finally {
167            try {
168               scope.close();
169            } finally {
170               recorder.record(name, tags, elapsed, error);
171            }
172         }
173      }
174   }
175}
